title: adonis apennina var. sibirica
intro: Adonis apennina var. sibirica, also known as Sibirian adonis, is a rare and highly toxic plant species belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The plant's roots and rhizomes are believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions by boiling its roots and rhizomes in water, but due to its toxicity, caution and proper guidance are necessary when using it for medicinal purposes.
